name: Flame
description: Create a small flame, light a torch, burn a rope, ignite oil, or hurl fire at one nearby enemy.

name: Fireball
description: Hurl a burning sphere that bursts among enemies.
combat_effect: Choose one target point. All creatures close to that point take 1d6 fire damage.
notes: A character caught in the blast may roll Grace, Challenge 12, to take half damage. Fireball is loud, bright, and may ignite flammable objects.

name: Lightning
description: Release a violent stroke of lightning in a straight line.
combat_effect: All creatures in the line take 1d8 lightning damage.
notes: A creature in the path may roll Grace, Challenge 12, to take half damage. Lightning may shatter glass, split wood, stun small creatures, or briefly light a dark area.

name: Ward
description: Give yourself or an ally magical protection.
combat_effect: Target gains +2 Defense until the start of your next turn.

name: Heal
description: Restore Life to a touched creature.
notes: A creature may benefit from Heal once per day.

name: Veil
description: Hide yourself or an ally in shadow, mist, or illusion.
combat_effect: Gain +2 to avoid being noticed for one scene.

name: Whisper
description: Send a short message to someone nearby, even through a door, wall, or crowd.
notes: The message must be short enough to speak in one breath.

name: Sight
description: Notice magic, hidden writing, spirits, curses, or unnatural danger for one scene.
notes: The spell does not explain everything, but it reveals that something unusual is present.

name: Command
description: Speak a forceful word filled with magical pressure.
combat_effect: A weak-minded creature may hesitate, drop something, back away, kneel, pause, or obey a simple one-word order.
notes: The Guide may require a harder roll against strong, intelligent, or magically protected creatures.

name: Unlock
description: Open a simple lock, latch, chest, or door.
notes: Strong locks, sealed doors, magical locks, or ancient mechanisms may require a harder roll. Unlock does not silence traps unless the Guide decides the trap is part of the lock.
